description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a predominantly white surface, marbled with subtle gray and brown veins. The rock has a rough, jagged texture with several angular facets and a few flatter surfaces, suggesting it may have been naturally weathered or broken from a larger formation. Its coloration and veining resemble quartz or marble, giving it a striking appearance that stands out in an urban environment. The surface is mostly matte, with some areas appearing slightly smoother due to natural wear. The rock's size and mass make it a substantial obstacle, likely used as a barrier or decorative element in parks or landscaped areas. There are no visible markings, graffiti, or signs of human modification on its surface.
description: A white, veined, rough-textured rock with angular facets and a substantial, natural appearance. Its marbled surface and irregular shape make it a prominent urban obstacle or decorative element.
description_view_0: This view shows the rock's broadest face, highlighting its white marbled surface and angular lower edges.
description_view_1: From this angle, the rock appears flatter and more elongated, with visible surface veining and a slightly sloped top.
description_view_2: This side reveals a more rounded profile, with the marbling and subtle gray streaks more prominent.
description_view_3: The rear view displays a smoother, less angular surface, with the white coloration dominating and minimal visible veining.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
